100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On February 25, 1926, a significant development in the political landscape of Europe emerged as the German government faced mounting pressure from both the left and right factions, reflecting growing instability. Meanwhile, in the United States, discussions continued regarding the enforcement of Prohibition, with increasing public sentiment favoring its repeal. Additionally, advancements in aviation were highlighted as pilots aimed to break records in transcontinental flights, capturing the nation's imagination.
